title: Prangos ammophila
taxonomy: Prangos ammophila belongs to the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It is a species of flowering plant native to Iran and other parts of the Middle East. Prangos ammophila is further classified within the genus Prangos and is closely related to other species within this genus. The exact classification of Prangos ammophila may also include the subfamily Apioideae and the order Apiales. Further detailed classification may be subject to ongoing research and updates in botanical taxonomy.
uses: Prangos ammophila has been traditionally used in folk medicine, specifically in the Middle East, to treat various health conditions. The plant's roots and seeds are believed to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. Traditionally, it has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as digestive problems. In addition, the plant has been used to treat fever and inflammation. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of Prangos ammophila for medicinal purposes.
